horizon_basis.scaling_principle: Sharpe-magnitude thresholds (those with units of standardized return) scale by sqrt(horizon_ratio) when forward-return horizon doubles. Rate thresholds (fraction-of-windows, count-min) are horizon-agnostic and remain unchanged. This is the standard random-walk-IID approximation: variance of N-period log return scales linearly with N, so sharpe (mean/std) scales with sqrt(N). Equivalent: sharpe at h10d is approximately sqrt(2) × sharpe at h5d for the same underlying strategy.

factor_evidence._horizon_unchanged_note: rank IC is rank-based, not sharpe-magnitude; positive_rate and quarter counts are counts/rates. All horizon-agnostic at this layer.

execution_stress._horizon_unchanged_note: participation rates are per-rebalance fractions; horizon scaling does not apply.

_sharpe_anomaly_h10d_note: Empirically calibrated: at h10d, individual walk-forward window sharpes routinely fall in 25-90 range (return-horizon scaling makes per-window mean/std ratios larger than at h5d). Pure sqrt-scaling of v10's 20.0 -> 28.3 quarantines 7 of 32 windows on v6_h10d (~22%) — too aggressive. Threshold raised to 200.0 as 'numerical pathology' floor: a 200 sharpe over 10-day window would imply zero return variance, which is not physically possible under cross-sectional rank-strategy. Single-window sharpes 25-150 at h10d are statistically real (heavy 10-day directional bias in some windows) and should NOT trigger quarantine.
